data.description:
## Why
The generic Pass-2 verify contract applies to EVERY finding. Two new binary sub-answers close whole FP/defect classes; one gloss fixes a false-premise gap. Because these touch the generic per-finding contract, they are designed and calibrated TOGETHER, not bolted on one at a time.

## What
- Add committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim {yes|no|insufficient} (G-4 + G-7): "Does a committed element (an AC, a decomposed task, a concrete edit, or a scope exclusion) depend on this claim being true, while the plan provides NEITHER a verification of it NOR a defined behavior if it is false?" Materiality gate = the phrase "committed element" (objective proxy for "matters"); keys on absence of verification-OR-fallback so bounded assumptions with a stated fallback correctly survive as fine. Serves G-4 exclusions, G-7(a) confident assertions, and G-7(b) hedges with ONE question.
- Add respects_artifact_altitude {yes|no|insufficient} (FP-1): "Does the finding demand detail or presume a choice that this artifact level legitimately defers to a child?" Kills altitude-mismatch FPs (implementation-level demands on a story). Complements applies_at (criterion-level altitude) at the finding level.
- Gloss evidence_entails_finding (FP-2): a finding demanding what the plan already states is a false-premise / null-delta finding -> not entailed. No new key.

## Scope
Both new sub-answers na-default so old sidecars stay comparable. participate in validity like existing sub-answers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim added to verifier contract + both variants, na-defaulting
- [ ] respects_artifact_altitude added, na-defaulting
- [ ] evidence_entails_finding prompt gloss for restatement/null-delta findings
- [ ] Calibration/eval confirms the well-formed ## Assumptions-block case survives as low-validity->dropped
- [ ] Generic pass stays generic (no criterion-specific branching in Pass 2)
data.title: WS1: Pass-2 vocabulary expansion (2 new generic sub-answers + 1 gloss)
